project.name: Proposal Generation Engine V2
project.description: Upgrade from wizard-based to pipeline-based proposal generation: one-click, context-rich, pixel-perfect PDF proposals via Gotenberg, with style fingerprinting, offering profiles, and all 4 trigger points

features.name: Style Fingerprint — Wire into Proposals
features.description: Connect existing user_tone_settings and analyze-writing-style infrastructure into proposal generation. Add edit-distance learning.

features.name: Offering Profile — Structured Product Data
features.description: New org_offering_profiles table, collateral upload/extract, wire into context assembly.

features.name: Gotenberg PDF — Pixel-Perfect Rendering
features.description: Deploy Gotenberg on Railway, build HTML template engine with CSS print media queries, replace pdf-lib.

features.name: 5-Stage Pipeline — End-to-End
features.description: Context assembly → AI composition → template merge → Gotenberg render → deliver. Full pipeline orchestration.

features.name: One-Click UX — Frontend
features.description: ProposalQuickGenerate button, progress overlay, copilot panel, proposals list view.

features.name: All 4 Triggers — Wiring
features.description: Update PROP-001 to V2 pipeline, consolidate copilot skills, update Slack handler.

features.name: Autopilot and Polish
features.description: Register rubber-stamp thresholds, edit distance tracking, QA, template refinement, performance tuning.

stories.title: Wire user_tone_settings into proposal-compose-v2 prompt
acceptance: Query user_tone_settings for the proposal creator's user_id
Inject formality, directness, warmth, words_to_avoid into AI system prompt
Default to 'professional, consultative, confident' when no tone settings exist
Style preferences visible in proposals.style_config JSON

stories.title: Extract style patterns from uploaded proposal examples
acceptance: When proposal-parse-document processes an upload, also analyze writing style
Extract sentence length averages, vocabulary patterns, tone indicators
Store writing style metadata alongside template in proposal_templates.brand_config
Style extraction uses Claude Haiku (cost-efficient, already used in generateCustomSectionsAdapter)

stories.title: Build compound style fingerprint from multiple sources
acceptance: Merge email analysis (user_tone_settings) + proposal examples + org defaults into single style_config JSON
Priority order: explicit user settings > learned from examples > org defaults
Output conforms to ProposalContextPayload.style_fingerprint interface from PRD
Exported as buildStyleFingerprint(userId, orgId) utility function

stories.title: Track edit distance on approved_edited proposals
acceptance: When approved_edited signal fires, compute Levenshtein distance between original and edited sections
Store edit_distance and edit_fields in ApprovalEvent metadata
Track which sections were modified (e.g., pricing edited 80% of the time)
Uses existing ApprovalEvent.edit_distance and edit_fields fields

stories.title: Style learning loop: aggregate edit patterns into tone settings
acceptance: Periodic aggregation of edit patterns per user from autopilot_signals
Identify trends: which sections get edited most, tone direction of edits
Update user_tone_settings with learned preferences (additive, never destructive)
Learning requires minimum 5 approved_edited signals before updating

stories.title: Migration: create org_offering_profiles table with RLS
acceptance: org_offering_profiles table created with columns per PRD Section 6
RLS policies: users can view/manage profiles within their org
Index on org_id for query performance
Migration is idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS pattern)

stories.title: Build OfferingUploader component for collateral upload
acceptance: Drag-and-drop file upload accepting PDF, DOCX, PPTX
Upload to proposal_assets with asset_type: 'document'
Shows upload progress and 'Analyzing document...' loading state
Follows same pattern as existing TemplateUploader component

stories.title: Create offering-extract edge function for AI-powered collateral analysis
acceptance: Edge function downloads file from Supabase Storage given a proposal_assets.id
Extract text via mammoth (DOCX) or pdf-parse (PDF)
Send to Claude for structured extraction: products, services, case studies, pricing models, differentiators
Creates/updates org_offering_profiles row with extracted JSON data
Credit cost: 1-2 credits logged via logAICostEvent

stories.title: Offering review UI: display and edit extracted data before saving
acceptance: Review screen showing extracted products, services, case studies, pricing models
Editable fields for each extracted item
Approve button saves to org_offering_profiles
Follows TemplateExtractReview pattern

stories.title: Wire offering profile into context assembly queries
acceptance: proposal-assemble-context queries org_offering_profiles for the org
Includes products, services, case studies, pricing models in context payload
Falls back to org_settings.value_propositions if no offering profile exists
Offering data respects 30k token budget (truncate if needed)

stories.title: Offering profile settings page: view, edit, delete, upload new
acceptance: New tab or section in Settings > Proposal Settings
List all offering profiles for the org with edit/delete actions
Upload new collateral button opens OfferingUploader
Active/inactive toggle per profile

stories.title: Deploy Gotenberg Docker container on Railway
acceptance: Gotenberg container deployed on Railway with internal networking
Accessible via internal URL (not public-facing)
Health check endpoint responds at /health
Configuration: A4 paper, 500ms waitDelay for font loading

stories.title: Build HTML template engine with Handlebars-style substitution
acceptance: Template engine merges ProposalSection[] into proposal_templates.content HTML
Variable substitution: {{deal.name}}, {{contact.name}}, {{section.content}} etc.
Section loop: iterate sections in order, render each into its HTML slot
Outputs complete HTML document ready for Gotenberg

stories.title: CSS print media queries for professional PDF output
acceptance: @page rules for margins, headers, footers, page numbers
@media print blocks for page-break control, orphan/widow handling
Print-specific typography (fonts embedded or loaded from CDN)
Brand bar, cover page layout render identically in browser and PDF

stories.title: Build 'Sandler Standard' default HTML + CSS print template
acceptance: Clean cover page with brand bar and client/company logos
Structured sections with consistent typography and spacing
Professional pricing tables with subtotals
Clear next steps section with action items
Page numbers in footer, header with company logo

stories.title: Create proposal-render-gotenberg edge function
acceptance: Edge function merges sections into HTML via template engine
POSTs multipart/form-data to Gotenberg (index.html, header.html, footer.html)
Receives PDF bytes, uploads to S3 (proposal-assets bucket)
Updates proposals.pdf_url and proposals.pdf_s3_key
Returns presigned download URL

stories.title: PDF first-page thumbnail for UI preview
acceptance: Use Gotenberg screenshot endpoint to capture first page as PNG thumbnail
Store thumbnail in proposal_assets with asset_type: 'image'
Thumbnail URL stored in proposals.brand_config.thumbnail_url
Thumbnail displayed in ProposalProgressOverlay Done state and ProposalPanel

stories.title: Deprecate proposal-generate-pdf (pdf-lib) with v1_legacy flag
acceptance: Add v1_legacy flag to proposal-generate-pdf
New requests routed to proposal-render-gotenberg by default
Existing requests with pipeline_version=1 still use pdf-lib
Deprecation warning logged on legacy calls

stories.title: Build proposal-assemble-context edge function (Stage 1)
acceptance: Queries all 8 data sources from PRD Section 3
Builds typed ProposalContextPayload JSON matching interface definition
Stores context snapshot in proposals.context_payload
Truncates transcript to 15k tokens; falls back to process-recording summary
Total payload stays under 30k token budget
Credit cost: 0 (database reads only)

stories.title: Build proposal-compose-v2 edge function (Stage 2)
acceptance: Accepts ProposalContextPayload + template schema + style fingerprint
Sends to Claude Sonnet 4.5 via model router
Returns ProposalSection[] structured JSON (validated against PROPOSAL_SECTIONS_SCHEMA)
Style calibration: injects style_fingerprint into system prompt
Offering awareness: maps products/services into Solution and Investment sections
Credit cost: 3-5 credits logged via logAICostEvent
Budget check via checkCreditBalance before generation

stories.title: Build proposal-deliver edge function (Stage 5)
acceptance: Creates activities row: type proposal_generated, linked to deal and contact
Sends Slack DM to rep with PDF download link
Updates proposals.status to 'ready'
If autonomy_tier is 'auto': sends client email with undo window
Credit cost: 0-0.5 credits

stories.title: Pipeline orchestration: chain stages with realtime status updates
acceptance: Chain: Stage 1 → 2 → 3-4 (GOT-005) → 5
Update proposals.generation_status at each stage via Supabase realtime
Store timing per stage in proposals metadata
Log credit usage per stage
Total pipeline completes in <60s

stories.title: Pipeline error handling and retry logic
acceptance: Graceful degradation at each stage (e.g., skip offering if not available)
Retry logic for transient failures (Gotenberg timeout, API errors)
Clear error states in proposals.generation_status = 'failed' with error_message
Uses existing retry pattern from REL-002 (exponential backoff 1s, 3s, 9s)

stories.title: Pipeline monitoring: timing, credits, error rates per stage
acceptance: Log start/end time for each pipeline stage
Aggregate credit usage across all stages per proposal
Store total in proposals.credits_used
Error rate tracking per stage for monitoring dashboard

stories.title: Build ProposalQuickGenerate button on meeting detail page
acceptance: Blue primary button 'Generate Proposal' with FileText icon (lucide-react)
Positioned next to existing action buttons on meeting detail
Disabled when meeting has no recording AND no notes, tooltip: 'No meeting data available yet'
Click fires V2 pipeline and opens ProposalProgressOverlay
Creates proposal record with trigger_type: 'manual_button'

stories.title: Build ProposalProgressOverlay with 5-stage progress bar
acceptance: Modal overlay with 5-stage progress bar per PRD Section 9
Subscribes to proposals table changes via Supabase Realtime
Each stage animates on completion with label and description
Final 'Done' state shows PDF thumbnail, Download PDF, Edit Sections, Send to Client buttons
Credits used badge shown in Done state

stories.title: Build ProposalPanel copilot response type
acceptance: New response panel type in the 48-panel architecture
Shows PDF thumbnail, title, client name
Quick actions: Download PDF, Edit in 60, Regenerate, Send to Client
Credits used badge and status indicator (generating / ready / sent)

stories.title: Build Proposals list view with filters and actions
acceptance: Table view: Title, Client, Deal, Created, Status, Trigger, Credits, Actions
Filterable by status, trigger_type, and date range
Sortable by all columns
Actions: Download PDF, Edit, Regenerate, Send, Delete
Accessible from main navigation

stories.title: Wire ProposalWizard as 'Customise' secondary path
acceptance: Secondary text link 'Customise' below ProposalQuickGenerate button
Opens existing ProposalWizard 8-step flow
Wizard pre-populated with deal/meeting context from the page
Wizard output routed through V2 pipeline for Gotenberg rendering

stories.title: Post-generation edit flow: Done → edit sections → re-render PDF
acceptance: From ProposalProgressOverlay Done state, 'Edit Sections' opens ProposalPreview
Inline editing of sections with existing contentEditable mode
'Re-generate PDF' button fires Stages 3-4 only (skip context assembly and composition)
Updated PDF replaces previous in S3

stories.title: Update detectProposalIntentAdapter (PROP-001) to use V2 pipeline
acceptance: PROP-001 calls V2 pipeline (proposal-pipeline-v2) instead of legacy generate-proposal
Pipeline receives deal_id, meeting_id, trigger_type: 'auto_post_meeting'
Existing skip conditions preserved (no intents, no deal_id)
Backward compatible: V1 pipeline still accessible via pipeline_version flag

stories.title: Consolidate 3 copilot skills into generate-proposal-v2
acceptance: New generate-proposal-v2 SKILL.md with frontmatter per PRD Section 8
Existing 3 skills marked as deprecated (is_active: false) but not deleted
Routing updated in copilotRoutingService.ts to map trigger phrases to new skill
Skill fires V2 pipeline with trigger_type: 'copilot'

stories.title: Update Slack proposal_request handler to use V2 pipeline
acceptance: Slack handler resolves entity ref to deal_id/meeting_id
Fires V2 pipeline with trigger_type: 'slack'
Posts progress updates in Slack thread (Stage 1... Stage 2... etc.)
Final message: PDF file upload + download link + 'Edit in 60' button

stories.title: Manual button trigger wiring (ProposalQuickGenerate → pipeline)
acceptance: ProposalQuickGenerate creates proposal record with trigger_type: 'manual_button'
Fires pipeline via supabase.functions.invoke('proposal-pipeline-v2')
Subscribes to realtime updates for ProposalProgressOverlay
All 4 trigger types now active and testable

stories.title: Register proposal.generate and proposal.send in RUBBER_STAMP_THRESHOLDS
acceptance: Add 'proposal.generate': 4000 to RUBBER_STAMP_THRESHOLDS
Add 'proposal.send': 5000 to RUBBER_STAMP_THRESHOLDS
Approvals faster than thresholds flagged as rubber stamps
Rubber stamps do not count toward autonomy promotion

stories.title: Wire edit distance tracking into autopilot signals for proposals
acceptance: When proposal approved with edits, compute Levenshtein ratio between original and edited sections
Store in ApprovalEvent.edit_distance (existing field)
Track edited section IDs in ApprovalEvent.edit_fields (existing field)
Signal weight: approved_edited (+0.3) applies to proposal actions

stories.title: Autonomy tier display in proposal settings
acceptance: Show current autonomy tier for proposal.generate and proposal.send
Explain promotion criteria (5+ approved for 'approve', 15+ with <5% edit for 'auto')
Allow manual override to a lower tier (never force promotion)
Display signal history summary: approved/rejected/edited counts

stories.title: QA: test all 4 triggers end-to-end with real meeting data
acceptance: Post-meeting auto trigger fires correctly on meeting_ended event with send_proposal intent
Manual button generates proposal from meeting detail page
Copilot skill generates proposal from 'write a proposal for [deal]' command
Slack handler generates proposal from '@60 write proposal for deal X'
All 4 produce valid PDF output via Gotenberg

stories.title: Template refinement: iterate Sandler Standard based on real output
acceptance: Generate 10+ proposals with real meeting data
Review PDF quality: typography, spacing, page breaks, brand rendering
Iterate CSS print media queries for optimal output
Fix any rendering issues across different content lengths

stories.title: Performance tuning: pipeline latency, Gotenberg warm-up, caching
acceptance: Pipeline p95 latency under 60 seconds
Gotenberg cold start mitigated (keep-alive or warm-up endpoint)
Context assembly queries cached where appropriate (company data, offering profile)
Pipeline timing logged per stage for monitoring

risks.description: Gotenberg container on Railway: cold start latency and reliability in production. Railway containers can sleep if not accessed frequently.
risks.mitigation: Configure Railway to keep container always-on (not sleep). Add warm-up ping. Fallback to Browserless.io cloud API if Railway has issues.

risks.description: Gotenberg network access from Supabase edge functions: edge functions run on Deno Deploy and may not have direct access to Railway internal networking.
risks.mitigation: Use Railway's public URL with API key auth, or deploy Gotenberg behind a reverse proxy with rate limiting. Test connectivity before building the full pipeline.

risks.description: CSS print media queries are notoriously finicky across different Chromium versions. Gotenberg's embedded Chromium version may render differently than expected.
risks.mitigation: Pin Gotenberg to a specific version. Test templates against the exact Chromium version in the container. Keep CSS simple and avoid bleeding-edge features.

risks.description: Context assembly hitting 30k token budget: 8 data sources may exceed budget for deals with rich history.
risks.mitigation: Priority-based truncation: transcript and offering profile are highest priority, conversation history is lowest. Implement token counting and truncation at assembly time.

risks.description: Offering extraction quality varies significantly by document type. Pitch decks (PPTX) are harder to parse than product sheets (PDF/DOCX).
risks.mitigation: Start with PDF/DOCX only (already supported via mammoth/pdfjs). Add PPTX support as a follow-up. AI prompt handles gracefully with partial extraction.

risks.description: Style fingerprint cold start: new users have no email analysis, no uploaded examples, no edit history.
risks.mitigation: Default to 'professional, consultative' tone. Prompt users to run analyze-writing-style during onboarding. Style improves naturally as edit history accumulates.

notes.v1Status: 28 stories complete. All Phase 1-4 features shipped. V1 plan at .sixty/plan-proposal-generator.json
keyDecisions: One-click primary UX, wizard as 'Customise' advanced path
Both email analysis + uploaded proposal examples for style source
Pixel-perfect branded PDF via Gotenberg (not pdf-lib)
Upload collateral + AI extract for offering data
All 4 triggers ship simultaneously in V1
notes.criticalPath: Phase 3 (Gotenberg) is the longest of the parallel phases → Phase 4 (Pipeline) → Phase 7 (Polish). Total: ~8 weeks with parallelization.
notes.existingInfra: PROP-001 (detectProposalIntentAdapter), PROP-002 (proposalApprovalAdapter), meeting_ended sequence, creditLedger, costTracking, user_tone_settings, analyze-writing-style, proposal_templates, proposal_assets — all production-ready and reused.
